cards.back: A self-contained unit of data transmitted over a network, containing source and destination addresses

cards.front: What is DVR in Unicast Routing Protocols?
cards.back: Destination-Dependent Routing (DVR) is a type of routing protocol that uses topology tables to determine the best path for routing packets

cards.front: What is LSR in Unicast Routing Protocols?
cards.back: Link-State Router (LSR) is a type of router that uses link-state routing protocols to determine the best path for routing packets

cards.front: What is PVR in Unicast Routing Protocols?
cards.back: Path-Vector Routing (PVR) is a type of routing protocol that uses path vectors to determine the best path for routing packets

cards.front: What is RIP in Unicast Routing Protocols?
cards.back: Routing Information Protocol (RIP) is a simple distance-vector routing protocol that uses hop count to determine the best path for routing packets
